title: How artifacts get their colors
description: Explanatory diagram of the artifact pipeline and its two theme modes — the file-to-embed flow, the site/self decision, where colors come from, and the verify gate. A live theme:"site" example: ambient chrome is injected, diagram strokes are local elemental color.

artifact.purpose: Give a reader or AI agent a one-page, diagrammed explanation of how this framework's artifact feature is scanned, embedded, routed, and themed — without reading the loader/route/script source.
artifact.interactions: none — static explanatory diagram; re-themes live only via the site's own light/dark toggle (no artifact-local JS)

elemental.note: Local diagram-content color only (flow arrows, branch markers, connector accents) — never used for backgrounds, text, borders, or spacing, which stay on the injected tokens above. Chosen at mid-lightness so each holds against both the light surface (#fafafa) and the dark surface (#0a0a0a) of the active theme.
